coverage_note: ECPL P&L workbook (1 Apr 25 – 28 Feb 26) is the main operating expense authority. Cleaned JSON lives under snapshots/. Regenerate ECPL Excel exports with: python scripts/export_ecpl_pl_workbook.py <path-to-xlsx>

datasets.title: ECPL P&L — main statement (cleaned)
datasets.description: All line items from P&L_Apr To_Feb_26_ECPL. Total indirect expenses, salaries, petty cash, and net profit are here — use as primary ECPL expense for the period.

datasets.title: ECPL — monthly expense breakdown
datasets.description: Rent, power, salary, petty, etc. by month (backing sheet for direct + some indirect lines).

datasets.title: ECPL — sales register totals
datasets.description: Sums from Sales Register sheet; gross sales must match P&L gross sales line.

datasets.title: ECPL — purchase register reconciliation
datasets.description: Explains PURCHASE ACCOUNT column vs Gross Total — ties P&L purchases to the correct column.
datasets.source_note: Do not sum Gross Total blindly

datasets.title: ECPL — indirect expenses paid by Nakul (detail)
datasets.description: Lines rolled into P&L indirect (Odoo, internet, Gmail, website).
datasets.source_note: Indirect exp_paid by Nakul M. sheet

datasets.title: ECPL sales — printed invoices vs P&L period
datasets.description: Compares P&L gross to printed invoice totals through 28-Feb-26 vs after (March).
datasets.source_note: Requires local CSV path when regenerating export script

datasets.title: ECPL workbook — export metadata
datasets.description: Source path, period label, authority note for ECPL expenses.

datasets.title: ECPL Axis — payments to Harshad / Nakul Me
datasets.description: Cleaned debit lines from ECPL Axis FY 25–26 extract (rent rule per your policy).
datasets.source_note: Manual snapshot — refresh when bank CSV updates

datasets.title: ECPL Axis — STPL (Sign Technic) credits
datasets.description: Working-capital inflows; not net ‘payable’ without ledger agreement.

datasets.title: Coverage checklist (sample)
datasets.description: High-level status rows for other streams.
